#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - GROQ API
#   - OpenRouter API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - groq: GROQ API via curl
#   - openrouter: OpenRouter API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Translate curl exit code to human-readable message
# Args:
#   $1 - curl exit code
# Returns:
#   Human-readable error description
_curl_error_message() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) echo "Success" ;;
        1) echo "Unsupported protocol" ;;
        2) echo "Failed to initialize" ;;
        3) echo "URL malformed" ;;
        5) echo "Couldn't resolve proxy" ;;
        6) echo "Couldn't resolve host" ;;
        7) echo "Failed to connect to host" ;;
        28) echo "Operation timeout" ;;
        35) echo "SSL connect error" ;;
        52) echo "Empty reply from server" ;;
        56) echo "Failure receiving network data" ;;
        *) echo "Unknown error (code $exit_code)" ;;
    esac
}

# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
_llm_log_completion() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local wall_time_ms="$3"

    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    fi

    # Build token component
    local tokens="unknown"
    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    fi

    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        groq)
            # GROQ uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for groq provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        openrouter)
            # OpenRouter uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openrouter provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, not used by Claude CLI)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    # $4 json_schema not used by Claude CLI

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI ($model) invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$output" ]; then
            log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        fi
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    _llm_log_completion "claude-cli" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Claude CLI ($model) returned empty result"
        log_error "Full response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenAI API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    # Build response_format based on whether schema is provided
    local response_format
    if [ -n "$json_schema" ]; then
        # Compact the schema JSON to single line
        local compacted_schema
        compacted_schema=$(echo "$json_schema" | jq -c .)
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_schema\", \"json_schema\": $compacted_schema }"
    else
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_object\" }"
    fi

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  $response_format
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    local http_code_file
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
    http_code_file=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -w "%{http_code}" -o "$http_code_file" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?

    # Read response body and HTTP code
    local response_body=""
    local http_code=""
    if [ -f "$http_code_file" ]; then
        response_body=$(cat "$http_code_file")
        http_code="${output}"
    fi
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup temp files
    rm -f "$payload_file" "$http_code_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        local curl_error_desc
        curl_error_desc=$(_curl_error_message "$exit_code")
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) call failed: ${curl_error_desc}"
        log_error "Curl exit code: ${exit_code}, HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$response_body" ]; then
            log_error "Response body: ${response_body}"
        fi
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Use response_body for further processing
    output="$response_body"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) returned error: ${error_msg}"
        log_error "HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        log_error "Full response: ${output}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openai-api" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) returned empty content"
        log_error "Full response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke GROQ API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Text content from GROQ API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (GROQ doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_groq() {

    else

    fi

    local payload

{
  "model": "$model",

    {

    }
  ],
  $response_format
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)

    local output
    local curl_errors

    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
    http_code_file=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -w "%{http_code}" -o "$http_code_file" \

        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?

    # Read response body and HTTP code
    local response_body=""
    local http_code=""

    fi
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup temp files
    rm -f "$payload_file" "$http_code_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        local curl_error_desc

        log_error "GROQ API ($model) call failed: ${curl_error_desc}"
        log_error "Curl exit code: ${exit_code}, HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$response_body" ]; then

        fi
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Use response_body for further processing
    output="$response_body"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "GROQ API ($model) returned error: ${error_msg}"
        log_error "HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        log_error "Full response: ${output}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # GROQ doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "groq" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from GROQ response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    # Build response_format based on whether schema is provided
    local response_format
    if [ -n "$json_schema" ]; then
        # Compact the schema JSON to single line
        local compacted_schema
        compacted_schema=$(echo "$json_schema" | jq -c .)
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_schema\", \"json_schema\": $compacted_schema }"
    else
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_object\" }"
    fi

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  $response_format
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    local http_code_file
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
    http_code_file=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -w "%{http_code}" -o "$http_code_file" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?

    # Read response body and HTTP code
    local response_body=""
    local http_code=""
    if [ -f "$http_code_file" ]; then
        response_body=$(cat "$http_code_file")
        # Extract HTTP code from end of output (curl -w writes it to stdout)
        http_code="${output}"
    fi
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup temp files
    rm -f "$payload_file" "$http_code_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        local curl_error_desc
        curl_error_desc=$(_curl_error_message "$exit_code")
        log_error "OpenRouter API ($model) call failed: ${curl_error_desc}"
        log_error "Curl exit code: ${exit_code}, HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$response_body" ]; then
            log_error "Response body: ${response_body}"
        fi
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Use response_body for further processing
    output="$response_body"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenRouter API ($model) returned error: ${error_msg}"
        log_error "HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        log_error "Full response: ${output}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openrouter" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenRouter response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, not used by custom provider)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    # $4 json_schema not used by custom provider

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"

    # Log timing information
    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    echo "$output"
}

# ============================================================================
# CIRCUIT BREAKER STATE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

# Get path to circuit breaker state file for current session
# Returns:
#   Path to state file
_circuit_breaker_state_file() {
    local session_id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-}"
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        # No session ID - use temp file (for tests)
        echo "/tmp/sidekick-circuit-breaker-$$.json"
        return 0
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(paths_session_dir "$session_id")
    echo "$session_dir/circuit-breaker.json"
}

# Load circuit breaker state
# Returns via globals:
#   CB_STATE - "CLOSED", "OPEN", or "HALF_OPEN"
#   CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES - failure count
#   CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME - epoch timestamp of last failure
#   CB_BACKOFF_DURATION - current backoff duration in seconds
#   CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - epoch timestamp when we can retry primary
_circuit_breaker_load_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Default state
    CB_STATE="CLOSED"
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=0
    CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
    CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=0

    # Load from file if exists
    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
        CB_STATE=$(jq -r '.state // "CLOSED"' "$state_file")
        CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$(jq -r '.consecutive_failures // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$(jq -r '.last_failure_time // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$(jq -r '.backoff_duration // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$(jq -r '.next_retry_time // 0' "$state_file")
    fi
}

# Save circuit breaker state
# Args:
#   Uses globals set by _circuit_breaker_load_state
_circuit_breaker_save_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Create parent directory if needed
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$state_file")"

    # Write state as JSON
    cat > "$state_file" <<EOF
{
  "state": "$CB_STATE",
  "consecutive_failures": $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES,
  "last_failure_time": $CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME,
  "backoff_duration": $CB_BACKOFF_DURATION,
  "next_retry_time": $CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME
}
EOF
}

# Check if circuit breaker should use fallback instead of primary
# Returns:
#   0 if should use fallback
#   1 if should use primary
_circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback() {
    # Check if circuit breaker is enabled
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 1  # Use primary
    fi

    # Check if fallback is configured
    local fallback_provider
    fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")
    if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
        return 1  # No fallback, use primary
    fi

    # Load current state
    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            # Circuit is closed, use primary
            return 1
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Circuit is open, check if backoff has expired
            if [ "$now" -ge "$CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME" ]; then
                # Backoff expired, transition to HALF_OPEN
                CB_STATE="HALF_OPEN"
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_info "Circuit breaker: OPEN -> HALF_OPEN (testing primary)"
                return 1  # Try primary in half-open state
            else
                # Still in backoff, use fallback
                local remaining=$((CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - now))
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: OPEN (retry in ${remaining}s)"
                return 0  # Use fallback
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Testing primary in half-open state
            return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_warn "Circuit breaker: unknown state '$CB_STATE', resetting to CLOSED"
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# Record a failure in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_failure() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$now
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$((CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES + 1))

    local threshold
    threshold=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD")
    threshold=${threshold:-3}

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -ge "$threshold" ]; then
                # Transition to OPEN
                CB_STATE="OPEN"

                # Calculate initial backoff
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                initial_backoff=${initial_backoff:-60}
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$initial_backoff
                CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

                log_warn "Circuit breaker: CLOSED -> OPEN after $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES failures (backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            else
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: failure $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES/$threshold in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Failed during test, go back to OPEN with increased backoff
            CB_STATE="OPEN"

            # Exponential backoff
            local multiplier
            multiplier=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER")
            multiplier=${multiplier:-2}

            local max_backoff
            max_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MAX")
            max_backoff=${max_backoff:-3600}

            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -eq 0 ]; then
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=${initial_backoff:-60}
            else
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$((CB_BACKOFF_DURATION * multiplier))
            fi

            # Cap at max
            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -gt "$max_backoff" ]; then
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$max_backoff
            fi

            CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

            log_warn "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> OPEN (test failed, backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Already open, just update timestamp
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: additional failure in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac

    _circuit_breaker_save_state
}

# Record a success in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_success() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Success during test, reset to CLOSED
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
            log_info "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED (primary recovered)"
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            ;;
        CLOSED)
            # Already closed, reset failure count on success
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -gt 0 ]; then
                CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: reset failure count in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Success shouldn't happen in OPEN state (we're using fallback)
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: unexpected success in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================

# Internal: Invoke a specific provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - prompt text
#   $4 - timeout in seconds
#   $5 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from provider
# Exits:
#   1 on error
_llm_invoke_provider() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    local timeout="$4"
    local json_schema="${5:-}"

    log_debug "Invoking provider: $provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        groq)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        openrouter)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support and circuit breaker fallback
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (optional if using provider defaults)
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, for response_format with json_schema type)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get primary provider from config
    local primary_provider
    primary_provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    primary_provider=${primary_provider:-claude-cli}

    # Get model (use provider default if not specified)
    if [ -z "$model" ]; then
        case "$primary_provider" in
            claude-cli)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                model=${model:-haiku}
                ;;
            openai-api)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                model=${model:-gpt-5-nano}
                ;;
            groq)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                model=${model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                ;;
            openrouter)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                model=${model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                ;;
            custom)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                model=${model:-default}
                ;;
        esac
    fi

    # Check circuit breaker
    local use_fallback=false
    local tried_primary=false
    local output

    if _circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback; then
        use_fallback=true
        log_info "Circuit breaker: using fallback provider"
    fi

    # Try primary provider first (unless circuit is open)
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "false" ]; then
        tried_primary=true

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$primary_provider" "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema" 2>&1); then
            # Primary succeeded
            _circuit_breaker_record_success

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "LLM ($primary_provider/$model) output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Primary failed
            log_warn "Primary provider failed: $primary_provider/$model"
            _circuit_breaker_record_failure
            use_fallback=true
        fi
    fi

    # Try fallback if needed
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "true" ]; then
        local fallback_provider
        fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")

        if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
            if [ "$tried_primary" = "true" ]; then
                log_error "Primary provider ($primary_provider/$model) failed and no fallback configured"
                return 1
            else
                log_error "Circuit breaker is open but no fallback configured"
                return 1
            fi
        fi

        # Get fallback model
        local fallback_model
        fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_MODEL")
        if [ -z "$fallback_model" ]; then
            # Use provider's default model
            case "$fallback_provider" in
                claude-cli)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-haiku}
                    ;;
                openai-api)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-gpt-5-nano}
                    ;;
                groq)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                    ;;
                openrouter)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                    ;;
                custom)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-default}
                    ;;
            esac
        fi

        log_info "Using fallback provider: $fallback_provider (model: $fallback_model)"

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$fallback_provider" "$fallback_model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema" 2>&1); then
            # Fallback succeeded
            # Note: We don't record success for fallback - circuit breaker tracks primary only

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "Fallback LLM ($fallback_provider/$fallback_model) output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Fallback also failed
            log_error "Fallback provider failed: $fallback_provider/$fallback_model"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - GROQ API
#   - OpenRouter API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - groq: GROQ API via curl
#   - openrouter: OpenRouter API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Translate curl exit code to human-readable message
# Args:
#   $1 - curl exit code
# Returns:
#   Human-readable error description
_curl_error_message() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) echo "Success" ;;
        1) echo "Unsupported protocol" ;;
        2) echo "Failed to initialize" ;;
        3) echo "URL malformed" ;;
        5) echo "Couldn't resolve proxy" ;;
        6) echo "Couldn't resolve host" ;;
        7) echo "Failed to connect to host" ;;
        28) echo "Operation timeout" ;;
        35) echo "SSL connect error" ;;
        52) echo "Empty reply from server" ;;
        56) echo "Failure receiving network data" ;;
        *) echo "Unknown error (code $exit_code)" ;;
    esac
}

# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
_llm_log_completion() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local wall_time_ms="$3"

    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    fi

    # Build token component
    local tokens="unknown"
    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    fi

    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        groq)
            # GROQ uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for groq provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        openrouter)
            # OpenRouter uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openrouter provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, not used by Claude CLI)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    # $4 json_schema not used by Claude CLI

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI ($model) invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$output" ]; then
            log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        fi
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    _llm_log_completion "claude-cli" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Claude CLI ($model) returned empty result"
        log_error "Full response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenAI API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    # Build response_format based on whether schema is provided
    local response_format
    if [ -n "$json_schema" ]; then
        # Compact the schema JSON to single line
        local compacted_schema
        compacted_schema=$(echo "$json_schema" | jq -c .)
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_schema\", \"json_schema\": $compacted_schema }"
    else
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_object\" }"
    fi

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  $response_format
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    local http_code_file
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
    http_code_file=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -w "%{http_code}" -o "$http_code_file" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?

    # Read response body and HTTP code
    local response_body=""
    local http_code=""
    if [ -f "$http_code_file" ]; then
        response_body=$(cat "$http_code_file")
        http_code="${output}"
    fi
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup temp files
    rm -f "$payload_file" "$http_code_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        local curl_error_desc
        curl_error_desc=$(_curl_error_message "$exit_code")
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) call failed: ${curl_error_desc}"
        log_error "Curl exit code: ${exit_code}, HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$response_body" ]; then
            log_error "Response body: ${response_body}"
        fi
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Use response_body for further processing
    output="$response_body"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) returned error: ${error_msg}"
        log_error "HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        log_error "Full response: ${output}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openai-api" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API ($model) returned empty content"
        log_error "Full response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke GROQ API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:

# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    # Build response_format based on whether schema is provided
    local response_format
    if [ -n "$json_schema" ]; then
        # Compact the schema JSON to single line
        local compacted_schema
        compacted_schema=$(echo "$json_schema" | jq -c .)
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_schema\", \"json_schema\": $compacted_schema }"
    else
        response_format="\"response_format\": { \"type\": \"json_object\" }"
    fi

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  $response_format
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    local http_code_file
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
    http_code_file=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -w "%{http_code}" -o "$http_code_file" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?

    # Read response body and HTTP code
    local response_body=""
    local http_code=""
    if [ -f "$http_code_file" ]; then
        response_body=$(cat "$http_code_file")
        # Extract HTTP code from end of output (curl -w writes it to stdout)
        http_code="${output}"
    fi
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup temp files
    rm -f "$payload_file" "$http_code_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        local curl_error_desc
        curl_error_desc=$(_curl_error_message "$exit_code")
        log_error "OpenRouter API ($model) call failed: ${curl_error_desc}"
        log_error "Curl exit code: ${exit_code}, HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        if [ -n "$errors" ]; then
            log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        fi
        if [ -n "$response_body" ]; then
            log_error "Response body: ${response_body}"
        fi
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Use response_body for further processing
    output="$response_body"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenRouter API ($model) returned error: ${error_msg}"
        log_error "HTTP status: ${http_code:-unknown}"
        log_error "Full response: ${output}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openrouter" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenRouter response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, not used by custom provider)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"
    # $4 json_schema not used by custom provider

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"

    # Log timing information
    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    echo "$output"
}

# ============================================================================
# CIRCUIT BREAKER STATE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

# Get path to circuit breaker state file for current session
# Returns:
#   Path to state file
_circuit_breaker_state_file() {
    local session_id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-}"
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        # No session ID - use temp file (for tests)
        echo "/tmp/sidekick-circuit-breaker-$$.json"
        return 0
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(paths_session_dir "$session_id")
    echo "$session_dir/circuit-breaker.json"
}

# Load circuit breaker state
# Returns via globals:
#   CB_STATE - "CLOSED", "OPEN", or "HALF_OPEN"
#   CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES - failure count
#   CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME - epoch timestamp of last failure
#   CB_BACKOFF_DURATION - current backoff duration in seconds
#   CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - epoch timestamp when we can retry primary
_circuit_breaker_load_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Default state
    CB_STATE="CLOSED"
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=0
    CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
    CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=0

    # Load from file if exists
    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
        CB_STATE=$(jq -r '.state // "CLOSED"' "$state_file")
        CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$(jq -r '.consecutive_failures // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$(jq -r '.last_failure_time // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$(jq -r '.backoff_duration // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$(jq -r '.next_retry_time // 0' "$state_file")
    fi
}

# Save circuit breaker state
# Args:
#   Uses globals set by _circuit_breaker_load_state
_circuit_breaker_save_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Create parent directory if needed
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$state_file")"

    # Write state as JSON
    cat > "$state_file" <<EOF
{
  "state": "$CB_STATE",
  "consecutive_failures": $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES,
  "last_failure_time": $CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME,
  "backoff_duration": $CB_BACKOFF_DURATION,
  "next_retry_time": $CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME
}
EOF
}

# Check if circuit breaker should use fallback instead of primary
# Returns:
#   0 if should use fallback
#   1 if should use primary
_circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback() {
    # Check if circuit breaker is enabled
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 1  # Use primary
    fi

    # Check if fallback is configured
    local fallback_provider
    fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")
    if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
        return 1  # No fallback, use primary
    fi

    # Load current state
    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            # Circuit is closed, use primary
            return 1
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Circuit is open, check if backoff has expired
            if [ "$now" -ge "$CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME" ]; then
                # Backoff expired, transition to HALF_OPEN
                CB_STATE="HALF_OPEN"
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_info "Circuit breaker: OPEN -> HALF_OPEN (testing primary)"
                return 1  # Try primary in half-open state
            else
                # Still in backoff, use fallback
                local remaining=$((CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - now))
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: OPEN (retry in ${remaining}s)"
                return 0  # Use fallback
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Testing primary in half-open state
            return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_warn "Circuit breaker: unknown state '$CB_STATE', resetting to CLOSED"
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# Record a failure in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_failure() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$now
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$((CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES + 1))

    local threshold
    threshold=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD")
    threshold=${threshold:-3}

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -ge "$threshold" ]; then
                # Transition to OPEN
                CB_STATE="OPEN"

                # Calculate initial backoff
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                initial_backoff=${initial_backoff:-60}
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$initial_backoff
                CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

                log_warn "Circuit breaker: CLOSED -> OPEN after $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES failures (backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            else
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: failure $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES/$threshold in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Failed during test, go back to OPEN with increased backoff
            CB_STATE="OPEN"

            # Exponential backoff
            local multiplier
            multiplier=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER")
            multiplier=${multiplier:-2}

            local max_backoff
            max_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MAX")
            max_backoff=${max_backoff:-3600}

            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -eq 0 ]; then
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=${initial_backoff:-60}
            else
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$((CB_BACKOFF_DURATION * multiplier))
            fi

            # Cap at max
            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -gt "$max_backoff" ]; then
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$max_backoff
            fi

            CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

            log_warn "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> OPEN (test failed, backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Already open, just update timestamp
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: additional failure in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac

    _circuit_breaker_save_state
}

# Record a success in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_success() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Success during test, reset to CLOSED
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
            log_info "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED (primary recovered)"
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            ;;
        CLOSED)
            # Already closed, reset failure count on success
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -gt 0 ]; then
                CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: reset failure count in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Success shouldn't happen in OPEN state (we're using fallback)
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: unexpected success in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================

# Internal: Invoke a specific provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - prompt text
#   $4 - timeout in seconds
#   $5 - JSON schema (optional)
# Returns:
#   Raw output from provider
# Exits:
#   1 on error
_llm_invoke_provider() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    local timeout="$4"
    local json_schema="${5:-}"

    log_debug "Invoking provider: $provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        groq)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        openrouter)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support and circuit breaker fallback
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (optional if using provider defaults)
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
#   $4 - JSON schema (optional, for response_format with json_schema type)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"
    local json_schema="${4:-}"

    # Get primary provider from config
    local primary_provider
    primary_provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    primary_provider=${primary_provider:-claude-cli}

    # Get model (use provider default if not specified)
    if [ -z "$model" ]; then
        case "$primary_provider" in
            claude-cli)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                model=${model:-haiku}
                ;;
            openai-api)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                model=${model:-gpt-5-nano}
                ;;
            groq)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                model=${model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                ;;
            openrouter)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                model=${model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                ;;
            custom)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                model=${model:-default}
                ;;
        esac
    fi

    # Check circuit breaker
    local use_fallback=false
    local tried_primary=false
    local output

    if _circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback; then
        use_fallback=true
        log_info "Circuit breaker: using fallback provider"
    fi

    # Try primary provider first (unless circuit is open)
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "false" ]; then
        tried_primary=true

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$primary_provider" "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema" 2>&1); then
            # Primary succeeded
            _circuit_breaker_record_success

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "LLM ($primary_provider/$model) output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Primary failed
            log_warn "Primary provider failed: $primary_provider/$model"
            _circuit_breaker_record_failure
            use_fallback=true
        fi
    fi

    # Try fallback if needed
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "true" ]; then
        local fallback_provider
        fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")

        if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
            if [ "$tried_primary" = "true" ]; then
                log_error "Primary provider ($primary_provider/$model) failed and no fallback configured"
                return 1
            else
                log_error "Circuit breaker is open but no fallback configured"
                return 1
            fi
        fi

        # Get fallback model
        local fallback_model
        fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_MODEL")
        if [ -z "$fallback_model" ]; then
            # Use provider's default model
            case "$fallback_provider" in
                claude-cli)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-haiku}
                    ;;
                openai-api)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-gpt-5-nano}
                    ;;
                groq)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                    ;;
                openrouter)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                    ;;
                custom)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-default}
                    ;;
            esac
        fi

        log_info "Using fallback provider: $fallback_provider (model: $fallback_model)"

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$fallback_provider" "$fallback_model" "$prompt" "$timeout" "$json_schema" 2>&1); then
            # Fallback succeeded
            # Note: We don't record success for fallback - circuit breaker tracks primary only

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "Fallback LLM ($fallback_provider/$fallback_model) output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Fallback also failed
            log_error "Fallback provider failed: $fallback_provider/$fallback_model"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
